title: Atolls of the Indian Ocean and Red Sea - Geomorphological Vector Data (MCRMP)
abstract: Shapefile-based geomorphological maps of coral reef atolls across the Indian Ocean and Red Sea region, from the Millennium Coral Reef Mapping Project (MCRMP), created by photo-interpretation of Landsat 7/8 imagery (Andrefouet, 2023). Distributed as per-region zipped shapefile bundles; only the Sudan bundle falls within the Red Sea bounding box, the remainder (BIOT/Chagos, Lakshadweep, Maldives, Seychelles) cover other Indian Ocean atoll systems but are archived together in this KAUST folder.

variables.name: geomorph_class
variables.label: Geomorphological classification (5-level hierarchy)
variables.description: Hierarchical geomorphological classification attributes (Level 1-5), plus LAND and REEF binary attributes, per mapped polygon. Field-level detail not verified - shapefile attribute table was not opened.

steward.name: Unassigned - KAUST Red Sea Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly downloadable from DataSuds (IRD) via the dataset DOI; no approval needed.

provenance.originator: Serge Andrefouet / IRD (Millennium Coral Reef Mapping Project), funded by NASA and IRD
provenance.license: Open data (DataSuds/IRD repository terms - not independently verified)
provenance.citation: Andrefouet Serge, 2023, "Atolls of Indian Ocean and Red Sea: geospatial vector data (MCRMP project)", DataSuds.

known_issues: n_rows values are the polygon/tile counts embedded in each zip filename (e.g. 'Sudan-2' = 2), not verified by opening the shapefiles.

Dataset spatial extent is much larger than the Red Sea catalog bbox (includes Chagos/BIOT, Lakshadweep, Maldives, Seychelles); bbox widened to roughly span from the Red Sea to the western Indian Ocean rather than clipped to Red Sea only, since all files are bundled as one MCRMP release.
Only the Sudan-2-ok.zip resource is directly relevant to the Red Sea subbasins; other regional resources included for archival completeness because they ship together in the KAUST folder.
Temporal range (2001-2007) taken from the readme's funding period description, not from shapefile metadata.
